MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Christine Witherspoon (Successor Trustee).

Ms. Ng stated that she did not know whether there is going to be a final account on the Trust, that Mr. Skelly passed away last September, that they were trying to coordinate all of the accountings (Margaret's probate/Thomas Jr. and Margaret's trust/Thomas Sr. trust) so that they dovetail and make sense, and requested a 30-day continuance. The matter was continued to June 1, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Trust Matter And Conservatorship Pcn-09-292959
Off Calendar Jul-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number PCN-09-292959 (CONSERVATORSHIP OF THOMAS SKELLY JR).

Appearances: Lillian Ng for Christine Witherspoon. Court-Appointed Counsel Bruce Feder for Thomas Skelly Jr.

Ms. Ng presented a background of the cases, stated that as to the conservatorship that things were going well, and that Mr. Skelly wants to appoint Ms. Witherspoon as health care directive agent for health care, that he's got the paperwork but has been reluctant to sign it, and that this would eliminate the conservatorship of the person. In addition, Ms. Ng stated that there are no issues with the family members, that no one objects to Ms. Witherspoon's services to this point, that the other issue in regard to the conservatorship of the Estate is that they thought they would marshall the assets and then file a petition for substituted judgment, that it hasn't been done because they've been busy with other matters such as Mr. Sheridan's fee petition considered by this Court on Monday, and that there are assets that just keep coming to their attention; sort of drifting here or there. Ms. Ng went on to address the Margaret Skelly Estate stating that she believes that there are two accounts in the wife's name only, that it is currently in Probate to put the pour over will into the Trust as well, and that Ms. Witherspoon has been appointed in that matter. Mr. Feder stated that things have calmed down since last year's drama. Ms. Ng addressed the tenant issue stating that Ms. Smith is gone, that the residence is vacant, that the Conservatee likes to go there once in a while, that they will just maintain it at this point, and that Mr. Kaplan's retainer has been paid. Mr. Feder stated that he would stay on to discuss the durable power of attorney with his client and then he would apply for discharge.

udge: Mary Wiss. Court Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR #11312. Court Investigator: Probate Director Mary Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
